**To My Dearest Daughter,**

I write with a trembling hand, the ink upon this paper is a little thin, but my heart is heavier than the storm that now rages upon the coast. Five years have passed since our last words, and I have not dared to write, for I have been a solitary sentinel upon this lighthouse, with only the wind and the waves and the memory of you. 

I have been a keeper of light, but not of heart. The sea is a cruel mistress, and the storm is a harbinger of doom. But tonight, it is different. The winds are howling with a fury, the waves are rising with a wrath, and the very sky is a weeping with a blackness. I have not slept, for I have been a watcher upon this tempest, and I have not dared to close my eyes, for I have been a prisoner to the storm, and I have not dared to think of you, but tonight, I have been a man upon the edge of existence, and I have not dared to write, for I have been a keeper of light, but not of hope.

I have not dared to write, but I have been a man upon the edge of existence, and I have not dared to think of you, but tonight, I have been a man upon the edge of existence, and I have not dared to write, for I have been a keeper of light, but not of hope.

**To My Dearest Daughter,**

**I sit upon the cliffs of this desolate coast, where the wind howls like the very devil, and the waves crash with a fury that would make a lesser man weep. The storm is comin, and I fear that I may not survive it. But I have writen this letter, not for the sake of the wind, but for the sake of the words that I have long to speak.**

**Five years have passed since our last conversation, and I have not been able to find the words to say. The world has changed, and I have become a stranger to myself, and I have not been able to find the strength to stand. But I have found the strength in this moment, and I have writen this letter, not to ask for forgiveness, but to tell you of the truth that I have long to speak.**

**You were once my pride, my joy, my everything. But time has been a thief, and I have not been able to find the words to say. The world has moved on, and I have become a shadow to you, and I have not been able to find the heart to speak. But I have found the heart in this moment, and I have writen this letter, not to ask for your forgiveness, but to tell you of the truth that I have long to speak.**

**I have not seen your face in years, but I have seen the storm in

**I write with a heavy heart, for I have not seen thy face in five long years, and I have not heard thy voice, nor felt thy touch. But tonight, a storm is coming, and I have not been able to find the strength to stand against it. and I have not been able to find the words to write to thee. But I write, for I have not been able to find the strength to stand against it, and I have not been able to find the words to write to thee.**
